title: Changes to how Kissflow Integrations look
html:
We're excited to unveil a fresh and intuitive look for Kissflow Integrations. This update will bring a host of enhancements to the integration builder aimed at providing a more seamless and user-friendly experience. Here are the changes:

Infinite canvas area

The integration builder will have an infinite canvas area, allowing you to pan across the canvas effortlessly. This ensures a more expansive and adaptable workspace for designing complex integrations.

You can easily locate and modify specific actions without the need to scroll through lengthy pages.

 

Redesigned side panel

Vertical display of steps: The horizontal sequence of trigger and action steps (Trigger/Action, Authentication, Configuration, and Output) involved while setting up an integration will be transformed into a vertical arrangement within a newly introduced side panel.

 

Redesigned list of connectors

When you add an action to an integration, all the connectors will be listed vertically. Kissflow’s internal connectors will be listed in the beginning, followed by the external connectors in alphabetical order.

 

Redesigned error panel

The error panel showing the list of errors will be shifted from the right to the left side of your screen. Clicking an error will take you to the action in which the error has occurred.

 

Unchanged integration functionality

While the look and feel will be revamped for a more modern and user-centric experience, there will not be any changes to the underlying functionality of your integrations. 

Expected date

Prepare to explore a more intuitive experience with Kissflow Integrations from the second week of January, 2024.

Stay tuned for the update, and as always, feel free to reach out to us in the comments section if you have any questions.

title: Introducing Print Attachments in Preview Mode on Kissflow!
html:
The highly sought-after feature is now live. Say goodbye to the hassle of downloading attachments for printing. We're excited to announce the release of one of our most requested enhancements: the ability to print attachments directly in preview mode.

Downloading files can be time-consuming and take up space. This new enhancement simplifies workflow and gives you more control over system space. This lets you print form attachments directly from the preview mode without downloading them to your device. Simply click the Print icon, and that's all it takes.

Note: We only support the download of PDFs, images, and text files in formats such as CSV and TXT.

We would love to hear your thoughts as you explore this new feature. Your feedback is valuable to us, and we are committed to improving Kissflow for you.

Log in to like this post and comment. We'd love to know if this could make a big difference and save you valuable time.

title: Action Required: Deprecation of Kissflow Employee Management Table
html:
In mid-January 2024, we will deprecate the employee management table in Kissflow. Post this, you will no longer be able to access employee details through this table.
 

Transition to User Management Table:
All relevant details, previously available in the employee management table, will now be accessible through the user management table. Please use the user management table to view and manage employee information.
 

Changes in Data Sharing:
While the employee management table allows users to share employee details with others, the user management table will have different access restrictions. Only User admins / IAM admins / Super admins will have the ability to access and view user details. This change is implemented to enhance data security and privacy.

Action Required:
Please familiarize yourself with the user management table. If you have been sharing employee details with other users, kindly inform them of this change and advise them to contact User admins / IAM admins / Super admins for any required information. Also, note that you will not be able to use the _employee() system field in the future. Existing fields with _employee() will be replaced with user() automatically.  Moving forward, you can use user() in your new workflows.

We understand that changes like these can impact your daily operations, and we appreciate your cooperation during this transition period. If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to reach out to our support team in the community. 

Note: This applies to users in the following plans - KF Digital Workflow, KF Process, KF Project, KF Workflow Lite, KF Workflow Suite, and KF Workflow.

title: Introducing External Data Object in Kissflow Apps
html:
Kissflow Apps users often require access to data from external systems. Previously, there were challenges in importing data from external platforms to Kissflow. 

We're excited to announce our latest feature, External Data Object, which enables Kissflow Apps users to directly access data from external systems without storing it in Kissflow. This means you can now effortlessly access data from third-party systems and integrate it into your Kissflow App without hassle.

https://vimeo.com/896461161/e49273a51b?share=copy

Once you've set up an external data connection, you can configure corresponding external data to make it accessible to various modules in your app's data layer, including Processes, Boards, and Data forms.

Using the current lookup field, you can look up data from an external system through the external data object you have set up. Moreover, Integrations offers an external data connector that enables you to send, update, or read data from the external system with ease.

Please note that this feature is currently available for MySQL only. We will eventually implement it with other third-party connectors based on user requirements.

Read more about this here. 

We will implement this feature in Kissflow Apps to customers on the enterprise plan on a need basis. If you're interested, please comment below. Our dedicated Kissflow support team will contact you to discuss this further.

Show your excitement by signing in and liking this post, and don't forget to share emojis or GIFs in the comments below!

title: Kissflow connector for BI
html:
This article discusses how Kissflow will share Snowflake data with Kissflow customers who have purchased the Kissflow connector for BI. The Kissflow connector for BI is a data integration tool that allows customers to connect their Kissflow account to Snowflake, a data warehousing platform. This allows customers to access and analyze their Kissflow data using a business intelligence (BI) tool of their choice, such as Microsoft Power BI or Tableau.

Provisioning the Snowflake account

The customer must have a fully functional Snowflake account before Kissflow can share the relevant database with them. Kissflow will simply share the customer's database with the customer's own Snowflake account. Compute costs, i.e., any costs incurred while analyzing data with Business Intelligence (BI) tools, are billed directly to the customer's Snowflake account. 

Note:

ETL, which stands for extract, transform and load, is a data integration process that combines data from multiple data sources into a single, consistent data store that is loaded into a data warehouse or other target system.

Steps taken by Kissflow

  1. Kissflow will perform an outbound sharing of the relevant database from Kissflow's Snowflake account to the customer's Snowflake account. (If an outbound share is revoked, a customer cannot access the database.)

  2. Nothing further will be done at the end of Kissflow except the data sync that occurs normally.

Note:

Note that customers with a .com domain must create their Snowflake account in the Google Cloud Platform (GCP) US Central1 (Iowa) region. Customers with an .eu domain must create their Snowflake account in the Europe West4 (Netherlands) region.

Note:

Note that the customer can have any Snowflake plan of their choice.

Steps taken when customer leaves Kissflow

  1. Kissflow stops the data sync and deactivates the outbound sharing.

  2. The customer should check the time travel data retention days with the Kissflow support team.

What can the customer achieve with the BI connector?

  1. In the customer's Snowflake account, they will check the inbound share and enable the Kissflow database.

  2. In their Snowflake account, the customer will be able to analyze the Kissflow data alongside data from their other databases. This type of combination analysis will assist the customer in gaining additional insights.

Kissflow Reports vs Kissflow connector for BI

Why do you need a Kissflow connector for BI when you already have Kissflow Reports? Customers frequently ask themselves this question. Here are some key differences between the two.

Feature

Kissflow Reports

Kissflow connector for BI

Best suited for

Real-time reports

Data over a past time period

Reports

Pre-built workload metrics

Workload analysis (completely DIY)

Report format

Tabular report only (child items as rows under the respective item)

Independent reports over child table and dataset

Hidden fields

Required for using in reports

Not required

Formula columns

Can be created in analytics, not in actual flows

Can be created in analytics

Reporting scope

Available within just one flow

Can combine one or more process, boards, child tables, dataforms or datasets

Bi-directional

Yes (click on a row in a tabular report to open the respective item)

No (uni-directional)

Access

Data, metrics, and insights only in reports

Data, metrics, and insights in BI tools

Need for analytics over built-in flow reports

  1. The ability to connect with BI tools like Power BI and Tableau directly, instead of exporting data as CSV or pulling data via REST APIs.

  2. Workload reports like User workload report, Time taken per step, Time taken per item, Deactivated users' tasks, etc. to analyze the workflow journey over time.

  3. The ability to create reports based on child tables in process, boards, and data forms.

  4. A consolidated view by combining child tables and datasets. E.g. Purchase request, Purchase order, and goods received note are all combined to form views used in procurement.

title: Important Announcement: Sunset of Kissflow Chat and Kissflow Spaces
html:
As part of our goal to provide the simplest-to-use low-code platform, we have decided to discontinue Kissflow Chat and Kissflow Spaces. Chat and Spaces were great collaboration tools, but we’ve decided we can better meet your needs by focusing all our efforts on building an AI-powered Low-Code No-Code (LCNC) platform.

Please note the following schedule for the sunset of these services:

  • Admin-only phase: Starting 20 December 2023, Kissflow Chat and Kissflow Spaces will transition to an admin-only mode. During this period, users will be able to access and view content but will not be able to create new content or modify existing content. However, admins can post communication. This will be useful for communicating transition plans or alternative arrangements.

  • Read-only phase: Starting 15 January 2024, Kissflow Chat and Kissflow Spaces will transition to a read-only mode. During this period, all users, including admins, can access and view content but cannot create new content or modify existing content. 

  • Service closure: Starting 1 July 2024, we will discontinue all functionalities of Kissflow Chat and Kissflow Spaces, rendering these services inaccessible. 

We understand this transition may pose challenges and are committed to supporting our users through this change. Our decision to sunset these services is a strategic step towards dedicating our resources and focus on building a more robust and innovative LCNC platform. Kissflow will be available in the Microsoft Teams, Google Chat, and Slack environments by Q1 2024 as part of this strategy. We are thrilled about the upcoming enhancements and possibilities, offering our users more options and an improved experience at their existing point of presence.

We appreciate your understanding and support as we evolve our services to meet your needs better. If you have any questions or require assistance, please do not hesitate to contact our customer success team or your account manager.

Thank you for your continued trust and support for Kissflow.

title: Introducing AI-Identified Sensitive Data in Kissflow Governance
html:
We understand the importance of safeguarding your sensitive information, be it employee data or details of external parties like customers and vendors. To ensure we offer you data privacy and confidentiality, we have introduced AI-identified sensitive data to Kissflow Governance, designed to prevent unwanted exposure to your sensitive data.

Accessible in Kissflow governance, this AI-powered feature highlights potentially sensitive fields to help manage sensitive data in your Kissflow forms and protect the confidentiality of your data.

The AI classifies form data based on the names of the fields provided. If these names are changed, our system will update them in real-time. This way, you can always stay up-to-date and keep your data accurate.

AI-Driven Categories:

Our AI diligently categorizes sensitive data into three types:

  1. Personal Data: Information identifying a specific individual, such as names, addresses, and contact details.

  2. Confidential Data: Information that requires heightened security, including proprietary business information, financial data, and login credentials.

  3. Health Data: Information about an individual's physical or mental health, medical history, or treatment records.

Below is an example of a medical appointment form with fields such as Name, Contact Number, Symptoms, Blood Group, and more. When you click AI Identify, our AI analyzes the field names and adds the relevant categories for each field. In the GIF below, you can see that details like name and contact number are categorized as Personal, while medical information like symptoms and blood group are categorized as Health.

Read more about this here.

Please log in to like this post if you found this new addition useful, and share your feedback in the comments below, telling us how beneficial this feature is.

If you have any questions on how to use this feature, feel free to ask in the comments below – we're here to help.

For Kissflow support queries, we recommend posting in the Get Help section. Rest assured, you'll receive immediate attention and assistance from our dedicated support team.

title: Configuring Google Workspace directory sync
html:
You can add users and synchronize user information from your third-party directory accounts to Kissflow using the Identity Provider feature under User Provisioning in Kissflow. We currently support user provisioning from Google Workspace (formerly called G Suite) and via SCIM.

An account can have only one user provisioning service enabled at a time, so if you are currently using SCIM, we recommend you contact our customer support team to help you disable it.

Prerequisites

Here are a few things you must know before you attempt to connect to Google Workspace:

  1. You must be a Super Admin, or an IAM Admin in your Kissflow account.
  2. You must be an Administrator in your Google Workspace account with the following permissions:
    • Organization: Read
    • Organization unit: Read
    • Users: Read
    • Groups: Read
    • Domain Settings
    • Domain management
    • Schema Management: Schema Read

If you are unsure whether you have the required permissions in your Google Workspace account, check this link to learn more about your role and privileges. Also, if your Administrator access is revoked in the future or your credentials do not work anymore, all the scheduled syncs will be disabled until you sign in back to your Google Workspace account.

Connecting to Google Workspace

  1. Access your Kissflow account on your web browser and click your profile picture > Account Administration > User provisioning > Identity providers > Google Workspace.
  2. Click Configure.
  3. Click Connect Google Workspace.
  4. Sign in to your Google Workspace account.
    1. Your connection might fail if you aren’t an Administrator in your Google Workspace account or if the account you tried to sign in wasn’t a Google Workspace account.
  5. Click Allow to let Kissflow access the information needed to connect to your Google Workspace account. This information includes your current role in Google Workspace, email address, domain, and users and groups under your domain.

You may now proceed to set up your sync as follows.

Selecting domains

After establishing a connection with your Google Workspace account, select the domains from which you would like to provision the users and their relevant group information to Kissflow. Click Next to proceed.

Selecting users or groups

After you’ve selected your preferred domains, you can choose to sync all the users from those domains or sync users only from a specific set of groups available in the selected domains.

Under User sync,

  • Select All users if you would like to add or sync all the users from the domains chosen by you in the previous step (or)
  • Select Users from a specific group(s) and choose the groups if you would like to add or sync users from the domains you chose in the previous step.

After selecting users, you can sync their group information by selecting the Add group information additionally checkbox. If you choose to sync users from specific groups, the selected groups will be populated automatically when you check this box. You can modify the list as you wish or leave it as is.

Click Next to proceed or click Previous if you would like to revisit the domains chosen for this configuration and modify the list.

Mapping and testing field attributes

Testing field mapping helps you spot discrepancies in the data between the source and destination services and avoid sync failures at a later stage.

By default, we will map three mandatory fields - First name, Last name, and Email ID from your Google Workspace account with their respective fields in your Kissflow account. Click + New field to add as many fields as you want from your Google Workspace account and map them with their equivalent fields in Kissflow.

After mapping fields, you can select certain fields for which you wouldn't want future updates to be synced into Kissflow.

Toggle Sync updates OFF to prevent a field from being updated in the future. However, disabling this field doesn't restrict the field from being added for the first time.

Let us assume an organization decides not to let its employees make changes to details such as their First name in Kissflow, but an employee changed his name from Michael Doe to Mike Doe now. If Sync updates is ON for the First name field, his new name 'Mike Doe' will be updated in Kissflow in the subsequent sync. If it is turned OFF, the changes to his First name will be discarded and retained as Michale Doe in Kissflow.

Click Test to preview the mapping results.

Your field mapping might fail in the following scenarios:

  • The source and destination fields must have the same data type. For example, you cannot map a ‘Manager name’ field (Text) in Google Workspace with a 'Joining date' field (Date) in Kissflow.
  • The field values must be in the accepted format. For example, the ‘Joining date’ field (Date) cannot have ‘June sixth’ as one of its values. Instead, it must follow the format 06/06/2019 (DD/MM/YYYY).

Resolve the errors, if any, and retest the mapping as necessary. Click Next to proceed to the next step when you are done testing.

Setting sync preferences

In this section, you can schedule sync, manage deleted Google Workspace users, and assign notification recipients for sync failures. Kissflow supports scheduled syncs only every week at the moment.

Note:
If your organization gets a new hire during scheduled syncs and wants to add them to Kissflow immediately, you may add them manually by clicking the Sync Now button in Account Administration > User provisioning > Identity providers > Google Workspace.

  • You can choose to delete or deactivate users in Kissflow when they are deleted from your Google Workspace account.

      
  • Select the recipients from the Notify sync failures dropdown to send them notifications in case of sync failure. The users will receive both in-app and email notifications in this regard.
  • Click Save & sync now to save your configuration.

Note:
Learn how to track the status of your sync, access sync history, and manage your configuration here.

Modifying configuration

  1. Click Edit configuration after accessing the configuration under Account administration > User provisioning > Directory sync > Google Workspace.

  2. Under Domains, add new domains by selecting their respective checkboxes or remove existing domains by deselecting their checkboxes accordingly.
    1. New users belonging to the newly added domains will be added and synced in the subsequent syncs. The existing users from the excluded domains and their group information will be deferred from being synced up the next time. However, their information will be retained in Kissflow.
  3. Similarly, modify the users and their group information in the next step as necessary.
  4. Test the field mapping again to ensure there aren't any data discrepancies.
  5. Modify your sync preferences as necessary.
  6. Click Save & sync now to save your changes and initiate the sync.

Can you log in as a different user when the configuration is active?

Yes, there could be instances when your credentials no longer work and you might have to log in again to avoid sync failures. Hover over your email address while editing the configuration to log out from your current session and log in again.

All the scheduled syncs will be disabled until the Administrator enters their Google Workspace account. Also, the existing configuration will be deleted permanently if you log in to a different Google Workspace account.

title: Revamping Kissflow Forms
html:
Hey there, Kissflow users! We've got some interesting updates in the pipeline that will make your experience with Kissflow Forms even smoother. 

A Kissflow Form is an entity used in processes, boards, and apps (data form) with which you can collect input from the people who participate in those workflows. 

Now, get ready for a revamped and more intuitive look for forms across Kissflow. Please note that this change will not affect any of the existing form behaviors. Here's what you can expect:

Common right navigation strip

The newly revamped form will have a common navigation strip placed at the right end, where you can switch between different tabs. We’re retaining the current details panel at the right, to show relevant information based on your tab selection.

 

Collapsible right navigation

With this change, you can maximize your screen space by collapsing the details panel to show only the navigation strip, giving you more room to work in your form.

 

Changes to process forms

Within process forms, we’re renaming the Widgets tab to Details for better clarity. As a result, the new right navigation strip will now comprise the Details and Comments tabs.

 

Additionally, we’re removing the capability to react with emojis on a process comment, although you can still like it.

Changes to board forms

We're updating the following board terminologies to enhance clarity:

  1. Transitions will be renamed to Status log.

  2. Activity will be renamed to Activity log.

The new right navigation strip of board forms will comprise the below tabs:

  1. Details - Will contain the system fields (Status, Assignee, Priority, Due date, and Requester).

  2. Subitems - Will contain all the subitems of the particular item.

  3. Notes - Will contain all the notes created in an item.

  4. Item log - Will contain the Status log and Activity log tabs.

We’re removing the Created at and Last modified information, as you can view that information in the Audit log and the Item log tab.

 

Quick access section panel

Post changes, the sections panel of process forms will not be static, and will now be accessible once you hover over the new quick access menu () in the top left side of the form. However, you can lock it if you want by clicking the Lock sections panel button () for added convenience.

Current form:

After the change:

We’ll be introducing the quick access section panel for the board forms too, making it easier to navigate to particular sections inside a board item. 

https://vimeo.com/881151493/f61743d0b9?share=copy

 

Scheduled date

These exciting changes will be rolled out tentatively in the last week of November, 2023.

We're confident these changes will make your Kissflow experience even more intuitive and enjoyable. Stay tuned for the update, and as always, feel free to reach out to us in the comments section if you have any questions.

title: Coming Soon: An enhanced App Builder with streamlined workflow and an advanced user interface
html:
Kissflow's App Builder is being redesigned and updated to offer a more user-friendly and intuitive interface, enhancing your experience. Here's a preview of the upcoming changes.

Remodeled navigation panel

A static navigation panel on the left will be present throughout the app-building process in the updated App Builder. This panel provides easy access to all necessary tools for app creation, such as pages, processes, and variables. For streamlined navigation, we've organized related modules into three categories: Data, Interface, and Logic.

Modular components

From now on, you can directly drag-and-drop flow components from the component library without first selecting a source flow. This feature will allow you to effortlessly change the source flow of a component without altering the page layout.
 

Uninterrupted development with tabs

Going forward, any feature you access in the App Builder will open as a separate tab in the builder window. This allows for seamless app development, letting you switch between tabs without losing progress.

For instance, if you’re working on a page and need to modify a process, you can quickly navigate to the process from the left panel,  and it’ll open in a new tab for convenient editing.

New features and style options

This revamp also introduces new features and styling choices for components, offering a range of customizations to help you tailor your app according to your specific needs.

The new and improved App Builder is all set to go live by the second week of December 2023. We hope you are excited to try out the enhanced App Builder designed to elevate your app-building experience.

title: Enhancing Access Control in Kissflow: Introducing Roles and More in User Management!
html:
We're excited to announce some significant changes to how access control functions within Kissflow. These changes are designed to offer you more flexibility and control over user roles and permissions.

Here's what's new:

  1. Optional User Role Assignment: User role assignment is now optional. Users can be assigned multiple roles if needed, giving you greater control over access.

  2. Introducing IAM Admin and App Store Admin Roles:

    • IAM Admin: This role manages access control within Kissflow and is responsible for security and user provisioning.

    • App Store Admin: This role oversees the App Store, ensuring apps are managed efficiently.

  3. User Group Role Assignment: Now, you can add roles to user groups, and all group members will inherit these roles, simplifying management.

To know more, click here.

We invite you to explore these changes and share your valuable feedback in the comments below. If you appreciate these enhancements, please show your support by liking this post. Remember to log in to leave a comment and like the post. Your input helps us improve your Kissflow experience!

title: Product Tip: How to comment on an item in a Kissflow process workflow
html:
To add a comment in a workflow, go to the Process and open the item that has been submitted. You can add your comments in the comments tab present on the right. At any instance, you can't edit and delete your own comments.

To notify a person directly, you can tag a person using the @ symbol followed by the person’s name.

Note: You can only tag the person who is a part of that particular workflow step.

title: All-New Improved Ways to Manage Kissflow Integrations
html:
We are excited to share that we have made significant improvements to managing Kissflow Integrations. It can be complex to manage integrations without access to detailed information, and navigating through them can be a hassle. That's why we have introduced a range of powerful capabilities to improve your experience by making it more efficient, user-friendly, and organized than ever before!

  • Search Functionality - You can now easily search for a specific integration name in our new search bar, eliminating the need to manually scroll through a long list.

  • Viewing Integrations - You can now view integrations in two ways:

    • Integration-based view - This view allows you to view your integrations in a list format. 

    • Connector-based view - This view lets you categorize and view your integrations based on connectors, triggers, and actions. This allows for a more customized and streamlined user experience.

  • Sorting Capabilities - You can sort the list of automations based on different parameters such as alphabetical order, date created, or status in a preferred order.
     

  • Automation Details and Metadata - You can quickly access all automation details without opening each integration separately. Information such as name, description, owner, last modified date, and status are readily available. 


You can read more about this here.

Please show us your excitement by liking this post and adding emojis or GIFs in the comments below. Let us know your feedback in the comments below. Your input will help us fine-tune this feature for an even better experience.

If you have any Kissflow support queries, you can post it in the Get Help section. Rest assured, you'll receive immediate attention and assistance from our support team.

title: Android devices
html:
If you are using an Android device, you can either use your browser to access your Kissflow account or use it as a Progressive Web App.

Using a mobile browser

Use your preferred mobile browser to navigate to the URL where your account is located. It will be <accountname>.kissflow.com.

Using a Progressive Web App (PWA)

A Progressive Web App gives a native app-like user experience, and it functions just like a mobile app. It also consumes less data and does not require any software updates. 

Note:

Minimum system requirements: 2GB RAM

New features are constantly being added to the PWA version of Kissflow; however, only a limited set of features are available at the moment.

Advantages of Kissflow PWA app

The Kissflow PWA app has the following advantages over a native app:

  1. Instant software upgrade - Unlike a native app, where software upgrades are delivered gradually over time, a PWA app's software update is smoother and more rapid.
  2. No device memory used - Every native app consumes a significant amount of device memory and processing power. This is not the case when using a PWA app that runs on your mobile browser.

Installing the Kissflow PWA app

  1. In your Android mobile app, open Chrome, Firefox, or any favorite browser and type your Kissflow account domain URL (https://<domainname>.kissflow.com) in the address bar.

     

  2. Tap the menu () > Add to Home screen and click Add.

  3. The PWA app will be automatically listed on the home screen of your android device.

 Enabling push notifications 

  1. Tap your profile picture in the upper-right corner.

  2. Tap the More options button ().

  3. Tap Notification settings.

  4. Toggle and enable Push notifications.

title: Product Tip: How to find the number of hours or days between two dates in a Kissflow form
html:
You want to find the number of hours or days between two dates. To do so, use the following formulas:

For hours, datediff(time_started, time_finished,"HOUR")

For days, datediff(time_started, time_finished,"DAY")

 For example, let’s have two fields called time started and time finished. In order to find the hour difference between them, create a third field called time spent and enter the formula datediff(time_started, time_finished,"HOUR") and click Save.

title: Introducing the All-New Expense Management App in Kissflow
html:
Are you tired of manually managing expenses in your organization? 

Kissflow is here to simplify your journey with our brand-new Expense Management app. 

Say goodbye to the stress of manually managing expenses and say hello to seamless and efficient expense management. Our latest app is designed to handle everything from employee reimbursement claims to overall expenditure reconciliation, ensuring an accurate expense-tracking solution. 

  • Expense Claim Processing: Handle various expense claims, ensuring a smooth workflow with our multi-tiered approval system.

  • Advanced Payment Requests: Simplify the process of managing advance payment requests from your employees, ensuring a streamlined approval process every step of the way.

  • Budget Allocation: Manage all expense and advance claims within your allocated budget, giving you complete control over your financial resources.

  • Hassle-free Travel Booking: Process and manage business trip requests, making corporate travel a breeze with user-friendly multi-tier approvals.

Our app also comes with a range of exciting features like expense reimbursement, multi-currency conversion capabilities, effortless bulk expense additions, insightful dashboards and reports, and much more, all designed to make your expense management journey smoother and more efficient than ever before.

title: Exciting New Updates in Kissflow Boards!
html:
We're thrilled to introduce three new enhancements to Kissflow Boards, designed to elevate your workflow and collaboration experiences!

  • Copy-paste Text to Create Items and Subitems
    Previously, creating multiple items required numerous interactions. With the latest enhancement, creating new items or subitems is faster and more convenient. You can easily copy and paste a text onto the board and click Create. This automatically creates a new item or subitem, eliminating the time and effort required to create an item from scratch.

  • Direct Link to Comments
    With the new comment link-sharing feature, you can now directly share comments from a board item's Notes section. This ensures your team can effortlessly share insights and keep the conversation flowing.

  • Effortless Subitem Rearrangement
    Organizing your workflow is now a breeze with the new enhancement that allows you to rearrange subitems within an item. Whether you're prioritizing tasks by due date, assignee, or any other parameter or simply customizing your board, this feature ensures swift and easy adjustments to your board's structure.

Your insights are invaluable in helping us refine and enhance your experience further.

title: Changes to List APIs - Deprecation of Aggregation Data

We're implementing changes to our List APIs regarding the usage of aggregation data. This update aims to ensure a seamless transition for our valued users.

Change details

Currently, customers have been utilizing List APIs with aggregation data. To accommodate this, we've recently introduced new dedicated APIs containing only aggregation data. This way, users have the option to use the new APIs if needed.

Upcoming change

We'll deprecate the aggregation data from the original List APIs after two weeks.

Impact

As a result of this change, the aggregation data will no longer be available through the original List APIs. Users are encouraged to transition to the new dedicated APIs for aggregation data.

title: Introducing AI Field Data Mapping in Kissflow Integrations
html:
Struggling with data field mapping in integrations? It's a common challenge that can consume time and effort. Navigating through fields from different steps and mapping them with multiple fields in actions often leads to less efficient workflow setups.

We're thrilled to introduce our latest AI-powered solution designed to streamline field mapping –
AI field mapping! Say hello to this feature that automatically identifies the optimal fields based on Trigger and Action outputs, making mapping effortless and accurate.

Our AI technology delves into identifying patterns, relationships, and transformations for precise mapping, ensuring a seamless integration experience. We use two types of mapping: the first type performs the best possible match across steps, while the other matches all possible matches across steps, allowing the user to remove unnecessary fields that were mapped.

Ready to simplify your data mapping journey? Try our AI field mapping and let us know how this enhancement transforms your work! If this feature has made your life easier, react to this post. Your feedback is invaluable to us. We can't wait to hear from you.

You can read more about this here. 

Login to like this post and comment. We would greatly appreciate it if you could let us know whether this new AI feature will make a significant difference for you and if it will help save you time.

title: Changes to export behavior in Kissflow Analytics
html: We're changing how data sources and reports are exported in Kissflow Analytics. Earlier, they were exported in a ZIP file. Now, you can directly export them in CSV or JSON format. This change applies only to Kissflow Analytics and does not affect flow reports in Kissflow.

title: Upcoming changes to access control within Kissflow
html:
In the next two weeks, we're rolling out critical changes to how access control functions within Kissflow. 

The issue: Kissflow currently manages access control via user types, inadvertently granting some users more access than intended. For instance, a User Admin, whose primary function was to manage users, also had access to billing data.

The solution: Moving from user types to user roles will ensure more granular and accurate access control of your Kissflow users.

Note:

All existing user types will be automatically mapped to the corresponding user roles. Ex: Super Admin to Super Admin, User Admin to User Admin. 

What’s changing?

  1. Optional role assignment: Unlike before, where every user was assigned one of the four user types, role assignment will now be optional.

  2. Multiple role assignment:  Users can have multiple roles assigned to them. For example, if you want a User Admin to have access to the billing page, you can assign them the Billing Admin role in addition to their User Admin role.

  3. New roles and updated permissions: 

    • Introducing IAM Admin and App Store Admin roles.

      • IAM Admin: Manages access control and is responsible for security and user provisioning.

      • App Store Admin: Can install apps from the App Store.

    • Shifting some permissions from User Admin to IAM Admin.

Note:

No permission changes exist for the Billing Admin and Super Admin roles.

title: Introducing Resource Manager in Kissflow Apps
html:
We are thrilled to introduce Resource Manager in Apps, a comprehensive file management system created to store, organize, and access visual assets within Kissflow Apps. 

With the Resource Manager, you can easily upload your visual assets from your local drive or external cloud services such as Box, OneDrive, Dropbox, or Google Drive and keep them organized inside folders within your apps. The resource manager feature allows you to upload up to ten files at a time, each with a maximum size of 5 MB. You can even create nested folders to organize your resources better. 

Once uploaded, you can directly refer to them from pages. You can also upload images directly from your local drive to pages. These images will be automatically stored in the root directory of the Resource Manager.

Here are some advantages of using Resource Manager in apps:

1. Consolidation: Provides a repository to store all app-specific images and icons, making it easier to manage them.

2. Speed and efficiency: Loads resources faster on pages since files are hosted within Kissflow. 

3. Organization: Lets you rename files, move them between folders, and create nested folders for further organization.

4. Quick and Efficient Search: Offers search and sort functions to help you find files quickly and efficiently.

https://vimeo.com/866371033/fa6de3ec61?share=copy

Note: The resource manager and its files are unique to each app within your Kissflow account. Hence, resources cannot be shared or accessed across different apps, even if they are part of the same Kissflow account.

Read more about resource manager here.

title: Exciting Updates in Kissflow Integrations for Enhanced User Experience!
html:
We're thrilled to announce that Kissflow Integrations has undergone some major upgrades and fixes! If you’re a frequent user of integrations, you may have already noticed these changes. In case you missed it, these updates aim to make managing integrations, connections, and your overall workflow a breeze. With improved data viewing and an enhanced run history, our updates offer a wide range of benefits. We hope you enjoy the new and improved Kissflow Integrations!

Check out what's new:

1. Optimized Space: The All Integration landing page is optimized to provide a more spacious and user-friendly layout.

2. Enhanced Data Viewing: The read-only grid component is replaced with a data table for better data visualization.

3. Empty State Improvement: The empty state display is updated for integration, connection, and run history sections for cases with no data available.

4. Simplified Navigation: The Create Integration/Connection button is now easily accessible in a circular tab section.

5. Integration Navigation Refinement: Several issues in the Integration navigation are resolved, including the error message that appeared when switching views. The connected apps column has been redesigned to provide a smoother experience, and disabled integrations are now displayed in a light gray shade.

title: Kissflow extension for Gmail
html:
View and approve your items in Kissflow from your Gmail by adding the Kissflow for Gmail extension to your Chrome browser.

Signing into Kissflow from Gmail

  1. Go to the Chrome web store and search for the keywords "Kissflow for Gmail". You can also directly access the extension here.

  2. Click Add to Chrome after accessing the extension.

  3. Click Add extension

  4. Open your Gmail. You should be able to see the Kissflow icon on the right navigation panel now.

  5. Click the Kissflow icon to sign in to your Kissflow account.

  6. Enter the domain name of your Kissflow account and click Submit to sign in. Learn how to access or switch between multiple Kissflow accounts using this extension here.

Managing your items in Kissflow from Gmail

You can view and approve your items in Kissflow from this extension. The My tasks section lists all the items which can be instantly approved by you, and the Tasks awaiting your action in Kissflow section lists all the items that await your inputs for approval.

Approving an item instantly

Under My tasks, scroll down to a specific task that you would like to approve and click Approve. If you need a second look at the task before approving it, click View task. You will be taken to your Kissflow account, where you can view the task in detail and act accordingly.

Accessing tasks that require your input for approval

Under Tasks awaiting your action in Kissflow, scroll down to a specific task that you would like to act upon and click Submit. You will be taken to your Kissflow account, where you can give your inputs accordingly.

Managing the extension

You can access multiple Kissflow accounts with the help of this extension. Click the account name under My tasks to go back to the main login page of the extension. Enter the name of another Kissflow domain and click Submit as you did before to access it.

 

Click the Refresh icon to reload your tasks in order to view the latest data from your Kissflow account, and click the Close icon to exit the widget. You can also remove an account from the extension by clicking the Trash icon on the main home page of the extension.

title: Boards FAQs
html:
Can I handle multiple use cases, such as issue tracking, help desk, and service requests using Kissflow Board?

Absolutely. Kissflow Board helps you build workflows to manage use cases such as project management, issue tracking, bug tracking, help desks, service requests, ticketing systems, and more - all on a single platform.

 

There are a few boards that I frequently use. How can I quickly access them without having to go to the Explore page?

You can pin these boards to the left navigation. On the board, click the icon next to the board's name. After pinning a board, you can quickly access them from the left navigation.

 

I’d like to add custom statuses to my board. Is that possible? 

Of course! Add any number of custom statuses that are relevant to your workflow in Settings.

 

What happens when I rename a custom status?

When you rename a custom status, the new name is applied across the board. Items that are currently assigned to this status will show the new name.

 

I no longer need one of the statuses in my workflow. Can I delete it? 

You can delete custom statuses you created from inside the Edit workflow menu. You can also delete all the items under that status or move them to other statuses before deleting them.

 

Can I customize the board form? If so, how?

Sure. You can customize the form so that it captures information that is relevant to your workflow. The form editor is very easy to use. Drag and drop fields or add new sections and tables according to your workflow’s requirements.

 

I no longer want priority for the items in my board, as this field is not applicable to my use case. What do I do? 

If you don’t want to use priority for your board, you can always turn it off in settings. You can do this even if priority was used earlier to create items or other features such as filters and views.

 

Can I create custom filters to track certain items frequently?

Sure. Use Views inside the board to create all kinds of customized views of your data. All views can be saved for future reuse. They will appear in the Views dropdown and can also be pinned as tabs.

 

I can see that items can be retrieved using different options such as ‘created by me’ and ‘requested by me'. Aren’t these the same?

Not quite. The view Created by me lists all the items you have created in the board. Whereas the view Requested by me, lists items that you have requested but may not have necessarily created. For example, if you are using a board as a help desk, you would often ‘create’ requests that were ‘requested’ by a customer. 

 

I want to find out the time spent on each status in an item. How can I do that? 

We’ve introduced a tab named Transitions inside the board form. All the status changes for an item are captured here. More importantly, you can view the amount of time an item has spent in a status.

 

Where can I find a history of actions performed on an item?

The Activity tab inside the board form. Open this tab to view a history of all important actions performed for it, such as changes made to values in system fields.

 

How do I determine the resolution time of a item? 

This is possible for any item that is in the Resolved or Closed status. Open any resolved or closed item, and you can view the resolution time under the Requester field on the right panel.

title: Introducing Enhancements in SAML Configuration for Smoother Setup and Control
html:
Dear Kissflow Community,

Exciting news about Kissflow’s SAML configuration! We've heard your feedback and addressed the challenges you faced while configuring Azure AD and Kissflow. 

We've refined the setup process for SAML configuration to ensure a seamless experience. No more worrying about mismatched orders, extra spaces, or incorrect slashes while pasting the configuration setup and SAML signing certification – it's just a click away. We've got you covered!

Smoother Setup: We've streamlined the setup process to make things more convenient for you. 

  • Downloading metadata is a click away – in your Kissflow account, select Download metadata, then upload it to Azure AD.
  • Upload the downloaded Azure AD Federation Metadata XML file in Kissflow by clicking Upload XML file.

 

All fields under IdP Configuration will be automatically populated, ensuring a seamless setup experience. And that's not all – for existing users who've completed the setup, data auto-populates in Kissflow's SAML configuration.

Visual Enhancement: Beyond the smoother setup, you'll notice a subtle yet impactful UI improvement, enhancing your interaction with Kissflow.

Email Notifications: Super Admins can now choose whether to send email notifications or not for SSO providers when enabling and disabling. This provides more communication control, allowing you to decide if users should be notified when SSO changes.

title: Introducing Custom Triggers in Kissflow Integrations!
html:
Hello Kissflow Champions! 

We're thrilled to introduce the newest addition to Kissflow Integrations - Custom Triggers. We understand that sometimes, setting up an integration with an existing trigger can be a challenge. With Custom Triggers, you can now create your own trigger for specific workflows. You can define input parameters and effortlessly apply them in subsequent actions. Moreover, this customizable trigger can be utilized as an action across numerous integrations.

For example, consider the Employee Onboarding and Offboarding processes tied to the Employee Dataset. Instead of managing separate integrations for these processes, custom triggers streamline it all into one.

 Benefits:

  • Ability to create a personalized input payload structure that aligns precisely with your needs.
  • Unleash the potential to associate a single integration with multiple events (e.g., Item Submission or Item Rejection), thereby overcoming the challenge of trigger redundancy.
  • Say goodbye to redundant integration configurations; no more need to recreate the same setup for different triggers.

Give it a try today and share your thoughts with us in the comments below. We eagerly await your feedback. If you're excited to give this feature a try, be sure to like this topic!

title: Send files and attachments easily with Kissflow's HTTP connector
html:
Hello there! 

We are excited to announce our latest feature that allows you to conveniently send files or attachments using the HTTP connector. This feature is exclusively available for the Custom action.

 Here's how you can do it:

  1. After configuring your trigger, choose Make an HTTP call (Custom) as the action and enter the URL to which the webhook needs to send the data. 
  2. Choose POST as the method, and add the relevant fields under HTTP headers and Query parameters if required. 
  3. Choose Form Data as the body type. Add the key name and map the respective field as its value. You can add multiple Key-Value pairs to the Form Data.

This feature is particularly useful for APIs that require POST data to be sent as Form Data. If you find this feature helpful and convenient, do like this post. For any questions, comment below and we'll be happy to help.

title: Upcoming changes: A revamped PDF template editor

What's changing?

In a few weeks, you'll see several improvements that address issues you may have encountered while generating PDFs. The new update will ensure that the PDFs you generate replicate the template you've set in the editor. Here's what you can look forward to:

  • Improved text area field alignment: We've resolved the alignment and positioning issues with the Text area field that were caused by tabs or spaces. You can use this field without experiencing any line break problems.
  • Responsive blockquotes field: The previously unresponsive Blockquotes field is now fixed. When specified in the template editor, the Blockquotes field will reflect in the final PDF with the appropriate formatting.
  • Optional user field: The user field will no longer be mandatory to generate PDFs. You can obtain PDFs of the processed forms even if the user field is empty.
  • Correct Thai letter depiction: We've fixed the problems with rendering certain Thai letters, which previously resulted in incorrect output.

PDF generated with current PDF template editor:

PDF generated with the updated PDF template editor:

What's new?

We're also introducing new elements to the PDF template editor:

  • New fonts: We're adding support for 30 new fonts, including PT Serif, Open Sans, and Playfair Display.
  • Image field support: We're introducing support for the image field in the header and footer of the PDF template editor.

We will be rolling out this update in a few weeks. Stay tuned for the official announcement and get ready to experience a more powerful and user-friendly PDF template editor.

title: Changes in SCIM User Provisioning Behavior
html:
Do you use System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM) in Kissflow for user provisioning? We recently made some behavioral changes to Kissflow’s SCIM-based user provisioning. Kindly take a few moments to read and understand this change. 

Previously, new users added using SCIM were ‘activated’ by default. There were various limitations to this approach to SCIM user provisioning. Specifically, if the number of new users pushed from your SCIM-based identity provider (IdP) to Kissflow exceeds the number of available Kissflow user licenses, only a subset of new users was added to Kissflow. 

As a result, users that were unable to be accommodated due to user license limits were omitted and were not added to Kissflow. Furthermore, Account Admins require control over who can be activated. The previous behavior prevented them from inviting all of their users via SCIM and then selecting and activating a handful of them.

Users who are added to Kissflow via SCIM will now be assigned the 'Invited' status by default, thanks to the new behavior update. Account Admins can then choose whether to activate all or a subset of the users added through the user management table.

We encourage you to check out our help docs to learn more about this change.

title: Data Sheet View in Kissflow Apps
html:

title: Introducing Service Accounts
html:
We are very excited to announce the release of Kissflow's Service Account feature. This feature is only available on Enterprise plans. You can create only up to 25 service accounts. Under each service account, you can create 50 access keys that can be used for access.

A service account is a non-user account used to automate integrations, access shared resources from outside your account, or approve Kissflow items on your behalf. It has an impersonation feature that allows you to impersonate a user and perform actions. Based on the use case, service accounts can access shared resources such as My Items, User and Group Management, Processes, Boards, Dataset, Integrations, and Chat. Each action by the service account is audited in a dedicated log, allowing Account Admins to govern them.

Ways you can use the Service Account:

  • Automate the integration between two processes so that the initiator of the item in the first process is considered the initiator of the second process rather than the owner of the integration.
  • Integrations authenticated with service account credentials run seamlessly and without interruption, but integrations authenticated with user credentials can fail if the user is disabled/deleted by her Kissflow.
  • Service accounts can automate certain aspects of Kissflow without requiring manual intervention. 
  • Programmatically access internal Kissflow resources without using Kissflow.

 To know more, read our help articles:

https://helpdocs.kissflow.com/service-accounts/service-account-overview

https://helpdocs.kissflow.com/service-accounts/creating-and-managing-service-accounts

https://helpdocs.kissflow.com/service-accounts/impersonation-in-service-accounts

https://helpdocs.kissflow.com/service-accounts/creating-and-managing-access-keys

https://helpdocs.kissflow.com/service-accounts/user-impersonation-using-a-service-account

title: Introducing Kissflow Portals
html:
We are pleased to introduce a highly requested feature: Kissflow Portals. This feature allows you to extend access to specific parts of your Kissflow account, such as apps or flows inside an app, to external users who are not part of your organization, such as vendors, partners, or customers.

Kissflow Portals eliminates the need to add external users to your Kissflow account, protecting the privacy of your internal workflows and communications, as well as the integrity of your business-sensitive data. At the same time, it gives you the flexibility to involve external users in specific parts of your workflows inside apps. Kissflow Portals are powered by the full capabilities of Kissflow apps, and offer a wide range of possibilities.

Customers on Kissflow Work Platform Enterprise plan will have access to create Portals with up to 10 external user licenses. To purchase additional external user licenses, contact us via the support chat.

title: Product Tip: How to transfer ownership of your account in Kissflow
html:
In Kissflow, only the current Account Owner can transfer ownership. 
Who is an Account Owner?

The Account Owner is the person who created the Kissflow account. They can access high-level administrative features like User management, Subscription & billing, Apps, and Reports. An account can have only one owner, but at any point, they can transfer ownership to a Super Admin. An account can have multiple Super Admins.

1) To transfer the account ownership, go to Account administration > Account settings > Account Owner and click Change.

2) Choose a Super Admin user from the pop-up that appears and click Confirm. 

After your confirmation, the ownership will be transferred to the Super Admin you selected. You will no longer be the Account Owner until someone transfers the ownership to you. However, you will continue to be the Super Admin in the account.

Let us know if you have any questions or feature enhancements on this, we’ll be happy to help.

title: Product Tip: How to export user or company data in Kissflow
html:
Customers need to export their user data as a report periodically and submit it to management so they can perform various analyses on the report for Audit purposes. Access to user management data is usually restricted to a select group of users to avoid unintended mistakes such as changing the manager or department of a user, which could result in disruptions in workflow assignment logic. 

In Kissflow, only Super Admins and User Admins have access to the user management table. Here’s how you can export user data: 

  • Click on your Profile picture > Account administration > User management 
  • Click on the Export user data button and choose between Filtered data (exports the details of only the filtered user records) and All data (exports the details of all the users in your Kissflow account).
  • Then choose the Format you’d like to export in.  
  • Once you click Export, you will get a notification and an email with a link to download the file.
  • When you click on the link attached to the email, you will be redirected to a new tab. Click Download to download the exported user data. 

You can learn more about exporting user data here.

html:

To set up a condition, find the workflow step and click Change. Find the section called When does this step happen?, and then click Change. Then, you can enter the formula.

For example, you may want to set a condition so that when a currency field in a form exceeds 1000 AUD, the form should be sent to Department Head Approval for Approval. However, if the currency is below 1000 AUD, then it should skip the Approval of Department Head Approval.

title: Product Tip: What to do when you’re unable to view the form in a shared report in Kissflow process
html:
As a Process admin, you can share a report and customize permissions for every member who has access to the report. By default, all the permissions are turned off for a member for security and privacy reasons.

However, if a member can see the list of all the items and you want to grant permission for the member to open the report, then go to More option in the report > Share > More option > edit the user's Permissions settings to View form. This will enable the member to view the form.

After creating it, open the text field and enter the Name as Instance id under Settings. Turn on the toggle switch under Is this a computed field?, enter the formula as _id and click on Done.

To delegate tasks, go to My settings > and click on Task delegation.

To enable task delegation, you can select a user under Who do you want to delegate your tasks to? select a Start date, End date, and click the Save button.

title: Product Tip: What does a suspended step mean in a Kissflow process workflow?
html:
While working on a process item, you may witness the term Suspended in a particular workflow step. A workflow step that has previously been present in the workflow but deleted later will appear as Suspended. Only an ongoing item in the in-progress state, when deleted, will be termed suspended.

In the image shown below, the Asset provisioning step was present in the workflow but deleted later. Hence, the step has been termed as Suspended.

To solve this, the Process Admin can reassign the workflow step to themselves and submit the form again.

title: Product Tip: What are audit logs in Kissflow and how to use them
html:
Kissflow has an account level audit log and a flow-level audit log that stores the history of all the activities performed in your Kissflow account.

Process level audit log: The audit log section stores the history of all the activities performed on your process, and the process audit log is available to the Process Admins.

On the process page, click the More options button > Audit log to access the audit log of the process you are currently using. The actions performed at the flow level will be recorded under Flow-level logs, and those performed at the item level will be recorded under Item-level logs. 

 Account level audit log: The account level audit log section stores the history of all the activities related to account settings, all user sign in, sign up events, actions, and high-level activities such as flow creation and deletion. 

The account level audit log is available to the Account Owners, Super Admins, and User Admins.

You can learn more about this here.

title: Product Tip: What is the difference between deleting and deactivating a user in Kissflow?
html:
Deactivating a user
When you deactivate a user from Kissflow, their license will be revoked but their data will be retained in the system.

Here’s what you should keep in mind while deactivating a user:

  • The deactivated user’s flow items must be assigned to another user by the Flow Admin. 
  • In case the deactivated user is a Flow Admin themselves, the new Flow Admin must be selected by the Super Admin. 
  • An Account Owner cannot be deactivated and therefore, their ownership must first be transferred to another user.

To deactivate a user: Select the checkbox beside the user and then click Deactivate user(s). 

Deleting a user
Deleting users removes them from your account. When you delete users, you lose all the data associated with them.

To delete a user: Select the checkbox by the user and click Delete user(s).

To know more, click here.

title: Product Tip: How to restrict the creation of new flows in Kissflow

  2. Under Who can create a new flow?, choose the flow for which you want to restrict the creation of new flows. 
  3. You will find three options when you click a flow: Anyone, Super Admin, and Anyone, after the approval of. Choose the User role that you’d like to grant access to and click Save.

Only Process Admin, Data Admin, and Reports Admins can create and edit the reports. If you are not a Process or Report Admin, you can request the Admin to edit your report according to your requirement.

title: Workaround: How to recover an accidentally deleted field or section from a process form
html:
As a process member, when working on a form, you may mistakenly delete a particular field or section. Once deleted, the changes are auto-saved; thus, you won't be able to recover these deleted fields/sections. However, if you haven't clicked the Go live button in the form, there's always a way to recover these deleted fields/sections.

To do this, you must request another process member with an Admin, Developer, or Data Admin permission to edit the process form to restore the deleted field/section.

When they click Edit process, a pop-up will appear asking them whether they want the changes to be discarded or saved. When they click "Discard their changes and take over editing", your changes will be undone, and the deleted fields/sections will be reinstated.

 Note: The above solution is just a workaround to be able to edit, recover or discard any changes to your form. We understand that some of you may not find this behavior efficient. However, we recommend trying this as a workaround for the time being. The product team is looking to bring an enhancement for this soon. 

Need more help? Drop your questions in the comments. We’ll help you out. 😁

title: Product Tip: How to restrict external users from submitting new requests in Kissflow
html:
Let's say your organization uses Kissflow Process to manage hiring. Since hiring involves receiving applications from external users, you can use the public form feature to host the process as an online job application form (public form). External users or, in this case, potential candidates can submit their application using the public form link which initiates an item in the Hiring process and triggers the Candidate Selection workflow step. 

But an organization may decide to freeze hiring for a period of time. In such cases, you don't want external users to initiate new applications in Kissflow. You can achieve this by disabling the public form.

Let’s take a look at how to disable a public form in a process: 

  1. Navigate to the process for which you would like to disable the public form.
  2. Click More options (...) → Settings → Public form settings.
  3. Turn OFF the toggle next to Public form URL to disable it. Once the public form is disabled, external users will not be able to initiate new items. However, the items that are already in progress will continue to move through the workflow steps.

title: Product Tip: How to format the output of a Number Field
html:
Let’s say you have a process named New Employee Salary Account Details which you use to gather the bank account details of new employees and set up their salary accounts. In order to collect the bank account numbers without any errors, you might use a number field. But since data in number fields are separated by commas by default, this isn’t an ideal solution.
Therefore, you can use a text field and add a validation that allows only numbers to be entered in the field. To do this,
1. Select the text field you wish to use as the bank account number field.
2. Go to Validation → Add a validation rule → select Numbers only.

This way, you can ensure that the employees only input numbers into this field. Every time an employee inputs a non-numeric value, they will see the following default error message: Value must be numeric.

title: Product Tip: How to delete lists in Kissflow
html:
Lists are single-column datasets whose data you can use in dropdowns, checklists, or checkbox/multi-select dropdown fields in your forms.

You can find and view lists by searching their names using Global Search. You can also find them inside forms while setting up a dropdown, checklist, checkbox, or multi-select dropdown fields.  You will find the “Select a list” dropdown in the field properties section when creating one of these fields. Click it to view the lists available. 

To delete a list in Kissflow, you must first be an admin of the list. 

When you open a list, you will find the list of processes that are currently using the list. Therefore, before deleting a list, you must ensure that any processes do not use the list. 

If it is used in a process, you will receive the following note when trying to delete it: This list cannot be deleted as fields are used in other flows. You'll need to remove those dependencies first. 

Once you’ve cleared the dependencies, if any, you can delete the list by clicking More options > Delete.

title: Product Tip: How to reassign an in-progress item in Kissflow
html:
You can reassign items to another approver if you are a Process admin or workflow step approver. 

Let's consider a Travel reimbursement process with two approval steps: Manager Approval and Finance Lead Approval. 

Once the manager reviews the bills, they approve the item and send it to the Finance Lead Approval step. Let's say the Finance Lead Approval step approver is on leave, and all their in-progress tasks are pending approval. 

In this case, the Process admin can click Reassign, choose the user they would like to reassign the item to in the Reassign to field, add notes and click Reassign. Also, to reassign an item that is incorrectly assigned to you, you can follow the same steps to reassign the item. 

Note that the reassign button is shown to approvers in the workflow by default. If you want to hide it from approvers, you can hide it by clicking Hide under Configure actions in Permissions for a particular step or all workflow steps.

title: Product Tip: How to import data in a dataset in Kissflow
html:
Watch this video to learn how to create a new dataset from a CSV file or import data from a CSV file into an existing one. 

Check if your CSV file meets the following requirements before importing them into a dataset:

  • Mandatory fields: If mandatory fields exist in your Dataset form, you must map at least one column in your CSV file with the required fields.
  • Data: Within the CSV file, you can map only the following field types to your dataset: Text, Text Area, Number, Rating, Date, Date-time, Currency, Yes/no, User, Single-select dropdown, Multi-select dropdown, Slider, Checkbox type fields.
  • Delimiter: You must only use the comma (,) as a delimiter inside the CSV file.
  • Rows: The CSV file can have up to 50000 rows, excluding the header.
  • Encoding: The CSV file must have a .csv extension that supports UTF-8 encoding. Note that if there are any failed rows while importing into an existing dataset, you can download the failed rows separately and view them. 

Note that if there are any failed rows while importing into an existing dataset, you can download the failed rows separately and view them. 

https://www.loom.com/share/8f4274bb87a0422bb9dd9008ea8b7d49

Learn more about importing data into datasets here.

title: Product Tip: Best practices for simultaneous tasks
html:
There are times in a workflow when you want multiple assignees to act on a step simultaneously. There are two ways to do this in Kissflow using Parallel branches or a Single step with multiple assignees. You may be confused about which one to choose. Let's see with a sample example. 

Use cases

The first is employee onboarding. At a certain section of the workflow, you want the IT, Finance, and Admin teams to act simultaneously as a part of the onboarding process. None of the steps are dependent on each other, but to move forward in the workflow, all of them need to complete. Each team has slightly different data you want to expose on the form and different fields to enter data into.

In this case, parallel branches are the right choice because you want the form details to appear differently. Also, if there are situations where a candidate doesn’t need to have all three tasks happen, you could write a condition to allow for that.

In the case of single step with multiple use cases, you need to get a purchase order approved by three people: the department head, the location head, and the purchasing manager. Similarly, they don’t need to happen in a particular order, but all of them must approve before moving on. All three will review the same information and provide their approval. The single step with multiple assignees is the right choice in this case. All of the approvers will always need to act, and they are all looking at the same data.

To know more, check out our help documentation.

title: Product Tip: How to use validations in Kissflow to create restrictions in your leave request form
html:
Here’s a quick video explaining how you can use Kissflow validations in your leave request form to restrict users from submitting leave applications when they fail to meet specific requirements. Learn how you can implement this in your organization based on your requirements.
https://www.loom.com/share/3b5810f8b24e4aa8a6fbfe58ca6bf673

  1. First, create a Rich text field and fill in the eligibility criteria details for personal and vacation leave so the employee knows the criteria.
  2. Then add a dropdown field with the different leave types and a hidden Date Limit field to determine the earliest date an employee can avail of a leave.
  3. To achieve this, we can compute the field using the IF() operator, with the formula if(Leave_type = "Personal," today() + 3, if(Leave_type = "Vacation," today() + 10, today())). This field will be computed based on the formula every time a new item is created. 
  4. Now, in the leave Start date field, add 2 validations: The date should not be in the past and should be Greater than the date in the date limit field.
  5. For the first validation, Set a custom error message: Start date cannot be in the past.
  6. For the second validation, choose the Date limit field and set the custom error message as: You do not meet the eligibility requirement. Please read the requirements mentioned above.

title: Product Tip: Do you have process items stuck for approval because the assignee left the organization suddenly?
html:
Consider a process with multiple workflow steps and an assignee for each step. We all know that an item can only go from one step to another step after the assignee approves it. Sometimes, there may be instances when an assignee in charge of a particular step may leave the organization without prior notice leaving all items under them unapproved. In such cases, all items assigned to them will be stuck in that particular step and will not move to the next step.

These items are listed under the Item reassignment tab inside Admin actions. In Kissflow, Account Admins or Flow admins can reassign these pending items to active users in the account to complete them. The Flow Admin can assign individual items to specific users one at a time. The items belonging to the user that was recently deactivated or deleted are ranked first.

The example below will help you understand how Account Admins and Flow Admins can reassign items to another user. 

Bucky has created a Leave Request process and the workflow has 2 steps:

  1. Manager Approval
  2. HR Approval

The Manager Approval step requires Loki's approval, while the HR Approval step requires Natasha's approval. Unfortunately, Loki leaves the organization, leaving all In progress items assigned to him incomplete with no one to act upon.

Only if Bucky reassigns another user to the Manager Approval step can the items under In progress be acted upon. Similarly, users will not be able to submit any new items. They will get the following notification while trying to submit a new item: There is no assignee for the next step Manager Approval. 

Here’s how you can reassign process items as a Flow Admin to another user. 

  1. Click My items > Admin actions > Item reassignment
  2. Select the Process item you’d like to reassign. 
  3. You will find the workflow step to be reassigned and the number of items waiting for approval in that step. 
  4. Search for the name of the user you would like to reassign to in the Search bar and click Continue. 
  5. Add notes or instructions in the Notes* section and click Reassign.

All items previously assigned to Loki will now be assigned to another user. https://www.loom.com/share/220a170687d2486ebe4bb39633c1095f

Read more about reassigning items here.

title: Product Tip: How to fix the “No assignee” error in Kissflow Process
html:
“There is no assignee in the next workflow step” is a  common error message that can come up in a process for various reasons.

The following are the possible causes and solutions: 

Cause 1: The assignee is an inactive user in Kissflow;

Solution: Process Admins can change the step assignee or reassign this item from the Global My items section.
Cause 2: The assignee is fetched from a User field or Email field in the form. If those fields are empty, the system will not be able to choose an assignee.
Solution: The Process Admin can make the field mandatory or select an assignee for the item from the Process Administration page. 
Cause 3: Inadvertent formula errors in the workflow step assignment options.
Solution: Process Admins can review the workflow step configuration and modify the formula.
Cause 4: The assignee field looks up data from a dataset that doesn’t have the required user information.
Solution: Process Admins can review the dataset and assign users to the field.

To know more, click here.

title: Product Tip: How can I delegate my tasks in Kissflow before I go on a holiday?
html:
The holiday season is about to begin, and before you leave for vacation, you need to plan for task delegation so that other users can attend to your tasks.

Here's how you can delegate your tasks in Kissflow:

  1. Click your profile picture in the upper-right corner of your screen and click My settings. You'll land on the Personal info page by default. 
  2. Click on Task Delegation. 
  3. Select a user under ‘Who do you want to delegate your tasks to?.’
  4. Select a Start and End date when you want your tasks to be delegated to the user.
  5. Click Save.
    https://www.loom.com/share/b0a5c2e510d349999ddc507cd6831645 
    You can delegate process tasks assigned to you automatically to another user. All existing tasks assigned to you before the task delegation starts will stay with you, and you can cancel the delegation at any time.
