title: Product Tip: What is a User Workload Report and how to create it in Kissflow
html:
A user workload report shows all in-progress process items assigned to users in your Kissflow account. As an Account admin, the user workload report helps track items assigned to different users in your account, identifies the number of pending items, and helps examine the overall workload of users. 

Benefits: Super Admins can decide to split the items based on the user workload. The Admins can see which person is overloaded with work and which one can take more tasks.

To create a user workload report of processes, you can use one of the following methods:

  • Create a tabular report using the ProcessInstance table as the data source.
  • Create a dataview and use it as a data source to build a chart or pivot report on top of it.

To know more, click here.

https://www.loom.com/share/7a480acd43a8426099e2a25f9c5e6e38

Users in the Work Platform plan will be able to create this report. Have questions? Do comment and we'll help you out.

title: Product Tip: How to create Document templates in Kissflow and email them through Integrations
html:
Here’s a quick video explaining how you can automate PDF generation using Document templates in Kissflow and email it through Integrations.

https://www.loom.com/share/1afb4fd76ca540b588f0736f191423ec

What is a document template?

You can use a document template to automate the generation of your process-related PDFs. You must be a Process Admin or a Process Developer to create these templates.

For example, suppose you are an HR manager and manage the ‘Recruitment’ process in your organization’s Kissflow account. In that case, you can create a document template that can help you generate offer letters automatically when the candidate progresses through the interview process and gets the offer.

When do I need to use it?

Here are a few examples of how different industries and teams can use this feature:

  • HR Team - Generate the employee offer/exit forms. 
  • Finance Team - Generate reimbursement bills 
  • Shipping and Logistics - Generate PDFs every time a purchase order is completed 
  • Educational Institutions - Generate mark sheets and certificates for the students. 
  • Quality Assurance - Generate certificates for brands 

Click here to learn how to create a document template from scratch. 

If you have any other questions about creating document templates, leave them in the comments.

title: Workaround Article: How to customize the number and currency format in Kissflow
html:
For long numbers, different symbols are used to group thousands and other denominations. The symbols used differ from region to region. Some countries use a comma (,), while others use a period (.).

The Number and currency format in Kissflow is set to Local (based on your browser settings) by default. Therefore, we have to customize the language settings in the browser to change the symbol used inside the form. 

Let’s consider a scenario where an organization in Chile, South America, uses Kissflow. Since the symbol used in the country is a period (.), we have to change the language settings in the browser to Spanish. Here’s how it can be done!

Open your Chrome browser settings, go to Languages and choose your primary language as Spanish (Chile). Reload the page for the change to take effect.

Now, when you enter numbers in the number or currency field, periods (.) will be used to group the numbers.

https://www.loom.com/share/25dbb7d8db7d41deb82d7cfe98a2507a

title: Product Tip: How to tag users in comments in Kissflow process forms
html:
In a process, once an item is submitted, users can add comments in the comments tab. You can attach files, add comments and tag initiators and the respective approvers of that particular step. You can only tag individual users and not groups in the comments. 

When a user is tagged in the comment, they will be notified via email and in-app. In the comments thread, users can reply to each comment separately and also react to them.

title: Product Tip: How to remove test data from system-generated reports in Kissflow
html:
Kissflow processes have system-generated metric reports that give you insights into your process. These are read-only reports; however, you might need to delete test or incorrect data from the report. Though you cannot edit the metric reports directly, you can go to More options > Administration and delete all test items you want to remove from the report. Deleting this will remove it from metric reports as well. This is how you can ensure that your reports contain clean and relevant data.

title: Product Tip: How to remove skipped workflow steps while printing a completed process item in Kissflow
html:
When you print a process form in Kissflow, you can also print the list of workflow steps in the form, including the steps the item skipped.

Certain approval steps may be skipped in a workflow based on certain conditions. When a workflow step is skipped, you can choose not to include the skipped steps while printing a form.

To ensure that all skipped steps are removed while printing:

Click the Print icon, enable Progress and select Exclude skipped steps. This will remove the skipped steps while printing.

title: Product Tip: Did you know that Kissflow chat supports voice messages that can be transcribed and translated?
html:
Check out this video to see it in action. 
https://www.loom.com/share/7d482bac57c64f1b80f4b3afe445dd16

Within the chat, you can record audio messages up to one minute in length. Tap the Record button next to the message box until the audio is recorded, then click the Send button to send the audio file to the user you're chatting with. You can also see the audio appear as text when you click the Transcribe button below the audio message. Once the audio message is transcribed, it can be translated into 50+ languages. Just select your preferred language and click Translate.

title: Product Tip: How to auto-populate the previous step name in a Kissflow process form
html:
To auto-populate the previous step name in a Kissflow process form, create a text field called Previous step name and toggle the 'Is this a computed field?' field to 'Yes' to enable it. Enter the formula lastCompletedStep().name() in the Formula tab, and click Done. Go to Permissions, set the visibility as Hidden for the Previous step name Text field, and click Go live.

When you open the form and proceed to the next steps in the workflow, the previous step name will be auto-populated in the form. 

https://www.loom.com/share/8aa55fa8553e47aea90461e37bc33937

html:

Note: Only a Super Admin and User Admin can access the User management system.

title: Product Tip: How to change my preferred language in my Kissflow account
html:
You may want to change the default language in your Kissflow account. To do so, click on your Profile image -> My settings > Format settings > Language > Click Save. 

Kissflow is currently offered in English, Simplified Chinese, Italian, Korean, Japanese, Spanish, Brazilian Portuguese, French, German, Russian, and Vietnamese.

These settings only affect your individual account and do not affect the entire account's format settings.

Help article: https://helpdocs.kissflow.com/admin/format-settings

Hint: If you are a Super Admin, you can change the default language for the entire Kissflow account. To do so, go to Account administration > Format settings > Language > click Save.
